title: Ike's Morning Digest
data_note: Built from publisher RSS metadata (titles + teaser text). `coverage_window_hours` is the default (40h); several feeds use a longer `rollingHours` (see `scripts/build-digest.mjs`) so rows align with what is still on each publisher’s listing pages. Commercial Observer keeps its retail category + hub supplement; TRD adds a New York–filtered global retail tag feed. PincusCo uses the live site feed only (`/category/retail/feed/` is stale). **CRE Daily** retail briefs come from `credaily.com/sectors/retail/feed/`. Open each link for the full article (subscriptions may apply in the browser).

section_overviews.commercial_observer:
Luxury Jewelry Brand David Yurman Takes 685 Fifth’s Entire 23K-SF Retail Condo: Luxury jewelry retailer David Yurman has leased the entire retail condominium at 685 Fifth Avenue , just one month after a $160 million loan on the property was transferred to special servicing.

Fred Astaire Dance Studios Leases First Park Slope Location: The international dance franchise Fred Astaire Dance Studios is waltzing into Brooklyn’s Park Slope neighborhood. The school’s new lease at 508 Fifth Avenue spans 2,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space previously occupied by shoe store Bobby Sportswear .

Thai Ramen Eatery to Open at Original Site of Danny Meyer’s Union Square Cafe: An Upper East Side Thai ramen joint is taking over a retail space near Union Square famous for being the location for Danny Meyer ’s first restaurant, Commercial Observer has learned.

Target Inks 135K-SF Lease at Queens’ Rego Park Shopping Center: Attention, Queens shoppers, you’re about to get another Target . The retail giant with over 2,000 stores in the U.S.
section_overviews.cre_daily:
Blackstone Lends $125M Refi to Dallas Mixed-Use Complex: Harbor Group International landed a $125M refinancing from Blackstone for the Alesio Urban Center in Irving, Texas. The 908-unit mixed-use property includes 55,500 SF of retail and recently underwent a $4.5M value-add program.

Shaver Hall Redefines NYC Food Halls For All-Day Appeal: FB Society opened Shaver Hall, a 35,000 SF food hall in Amazon’s Midtown NYC office, aiming to be an all-day destination. This project features 11 eateries, three restaurants, two bars, and extended hours, blending dining with entertainment and nightlife to boost foot traffic.

Jersey Mike’s Aims for $12B IPO, 15,000 Global Locations: Jersey Mike’s plans a public market debut and targets 15,000 stores worldwide, more than quadrupling its current footprint. The chain has entered a 300-store Canadian development deal and detailed a UK/Ireland expansion as it capitalizes on franchisee momentum.

Inflation Pushes Back-to-School Shoppers to Discount Retail: Average back-to-school spending is up nearly 12% to $489 per child, outpacing inflation, according to JLL’s 2026 Back-to-School Survey. Dollar stores cracked the top 10 list of most-visited stores for school shoppers, with 90% of parents planning to shop in-store and 77% naming Walmart as their top choice.
section_overviews.new_york_post:
Mysterious Manhattan building up for sale for first time in more than a decade: RFR developer Aby Rosen, who bought spooky 190 Bowery in a $55 million in 2015, tapped Avison Young’s James Nelson to offer the six-story property with 33,000 square feet of office space.

Uptown retail booming on Manhattan’s Second Avenue: The opening of the Q subway line and rezoning a decade ago propelled a residential development frenzy, including at least seven major projects between East 71st and East 86th streets.

Billionaires’ Row tower signs new tenant: The deal for two full floors brings the project to nearly 75% leased less than three years since construction began.

Elisha Cuthbert finds a buyer for her LA home as she returns to acting following a 4-year hiatus: The storybook residence, a traditional home built in 1936, stands just up the hill from Chateau Marmont, the legendary hotel in West Hollywood.
section_overviews.pincusco:
Sonesta International signs $245M refi with Fortress for four hotels in Manhattan: Sonesta International Hotels through the entity Sonesta Benjamin Hotel LLC as borrower signed a refi.

CW Realty in contract to pay undisclosed amount for Crown Heights dev site, plans 99 units: CW Realty Group through the entity 1062 Atlantic Member LLC is in contract to pay.

Quinlan Development files plans for 24-story residential in Fort Greene: Quinlan Development Group submitted a new building construction project for a 159,007-square-foot residential (R-2) building.
section_overviews.the_real_deal:
Target inks largest June NYC retail lease: The biggest retail lease in NYC last month truly dwarfs the rest of the list, coming in at more than five times the square footage of the next biggest lease on the list. Below is the full list of New York’s biggest retail leases in June.

NYC’s top deals: Brooklyn logs borough-wide record for year with $25M townhouse sale: There were 231 transactions totaling $338 million filed in New York City records in the 24 hours before 4 p.m. on Monday, July 6.

Vanbarton reels in $352M refi for nearly full Grand Central office tower: Vanbarton Group landed a fresh nine-figure loan for its nearly full Midtown office tower. Goldman Sachs provided a $352 million loan for 425 Lexington Avenue, a 31-story, 747,000-square-foot Class A office building next to Grand Central Terminal, according to property records.

Azorim secures financing for final phase of Yonkers complex: An Israel-based development firm is preparing to put the finishing touches on a massive multifamily complex a dozen miles north of Manhattan. Azorim landed $69 million in construction financing from Western Alliance Bank for the fourth and final building of Mizora at Ridge Hill in Yonkers, the Commercial Observer.

items.headline: Luxury Jewelry Brand David Yurman Takes 685 Fifth’s Entire 23K-SF Retail Condo
items.summary: Luxury jewelry retailer David Yurman has leased the entire retail condominium at 685 Fifth Avenue , just one month after a $160 million loan on the property was transferred to special servicing. The jewelry brand will open a new 23,000-square-foot David Yurman flagship store at the base of the 29-story Midtown building, according to landlord GGP , the retail subsidiary of Brookfield Corporation .…

items.headline: Fred Astaire Dance Studios Leases First Park Slope Location
items.summary: The international dance franchise Fred Astaire Dance Studios is waltzing into Brooklyn’s Park Slope neighborhood. The school’s new lease at 508 Fifth Avenue spans 2,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space previously occupied by shoe store Bobby Sportswear . The 10-year deal’s asking rent was $72 per square foot. The lease is the New York dance studio franchise’s sixth deal completed by Lee & Associates NYC ‘s Claude Kolb .…

items.headline: Thai Ramen Eatery to Open at Original Site of Danny Meyer’s Union Square Cafe
items.summary: An Upper East Side Thai ramen joint is taking over a retail space near Union Square famous for being the location for Danny Meyer ’s first restaurant, Commercial Observer has learned. Bua Thai Ramen & Robata Grill has signed a 4,840-square-foot lease at David Ellis Real Estate ’s 21 East 16th Street in an expansion of its current presence at 1611 Second Avenue , according to landlord broker Retail by MONA . The lease is for 10 years and asking rent for the space was $250 per square foot, the brokerage said.…

items.headline: Blackstone Lends $125M Refi to Dallas Mixed-Use Complex
items.summary: Harbor Group International landed a $125M refinancing from Blackstone for the Alesio Urban Center in Irving, Texas. The 908-unit mixed-use property includes 55,500 SF of retail and recently underwent a $4.5M value-add program. The deal underscores lender appetite for stable, large-scale multifamily assets in the Dallas market despite national debt market volatility.…

items.headline: Target inks largest June NYC retail lease
items.summary: The biggest retail lease in NYC last month truly dwarfs the rest of the list, coming in at more than five times the square footage of the next biggest lease on the list. Below is the full list of New York’s biggest retail leases in June. 1) Target | 61-35 Junction Boulevard | Rego Park | 135,000 sf The retail giant signed a new lease in the Rego Park Shopping Center, which totals 600,000 square feet of space.…

items.headline: Target Inks 135K-SF Lease at Queens’ Rego Park Shopping Center
items.summary: Attention, Queens shoppers, you’re about to get another Target . The retail giant with over 2,000 stores in the U.S. is about to add another location in the busy borough of Queens. Target inked a 15-year, 135,000-square-foot lease at the Rego Park Shopping Center at 61-35 Junction Boulevard in Rego Park, property owner Alexander’s announced Monday. The Rego Park Shopping Center is a multilevel, 600,000-square-foot open-air mall anchored by wholesale giant Costco .…

items.headline: Development Firm InvestMates Buys Greenpoint Retail Building for $26M
items.summary: Development firm Investmates has purchased a single-story retail building in Greenpoint, Brooklyn, Commercial Observer has learned. The company acquired 150 Green Street for approximately $26 million from foreign investors a week after revealing that it landed a $21 million loan from Northwind Group for the acquisition. InvestMates plans to build a 73-unit condominium building with ground-floor retail at the location, according to a source with knowledge of the deal.…

items.headline: Shaver Hall Redefines NYC Food Halls For All-Day Appeal
items.summary: FB Society opened Shaver Hall, a 35,000 SF food hall in Amazon’s Midtown NYC office, aiming to be an all-day destination. This project features 11 eateries, three restaurants, two bars, and extended hours, blending dining with entertainment and nightlife to boost foot traffic. The model signals a national pivot as food halls embrace longer hours, event programming, and adaptive reuse strategies for mixed-use viability.…

items.headline: Jersey Mike’s Aims for $12B IPO, 15,000 Global Locations
items.summary: Jersey Mike’s plans a public market debut and targets 15,000 stores worldwide, more than quadrupling its current footprint. The chain has entered a 300-store Canadian development deal and detailed a UK/Ireland expansion as it capitalizes on franchisee momentum. Its strong growth ambitions echo a broader IPO wave across the restaurant and retail sectors, signaling continued investor interest in global rollouts.…

items.headline: Korean Chef Jiho Kim to Open Flagship Restaurant at 21 West 17th Street
items.summary: Korean chef Jiho Kim has leased 3,120 square feet at 21 West 17th Street in Manhattan’s Flatiron District for his namesake flagship restaurant, Jiho . The New American eatery with a Korean-inspired menu will take up 1,868 square feet on the ground floor and 1,252 square feet of basement space at the Flatiron location between Fifth Avenue and Avenue of the Americas, Commercial Observer has learned. Asking rent was $120 per square foot.…

items.headline: Inflation Pushes Back-to-School Shoppers to Discount Retail
items.summary: Average back-to-school spending is up nearly 12% to $489 per child, outpacing inflation, according to JLL’s 2026 Back-to-School Survey. Dollar stores cracked the top 10 list of most-visited stores for school shoppers, with 90% of parents planning to shop in-store and 77% naming Walmart as their top choice. The shift toward value-driven retail benefits shopping centers anchored by discount and mass merchants, driving up physical retail foot traffic during the July spending surge.…

items.headline: SoHo Tops US Trophy Retail Sales With $195M in Q1 Deals
items.summary: SoHo accounted for 43% of trophy retail trades tracked in Q1 2026, leading all US shopping corridors with $195M in sales volume, per ACP. Foreign buyers and brand owner-occupants drove activity, targeting boutique trophy assets in the $10M–$50M range as hedge against rising rents. Rising rents and low vacancy are prompting luxury brands to acquire their flagship locations outright in multiple gateway markets.…

items.headline: World Cup Tourists Flock to Buc-ee’s, Walmart, and Waffle House
items.summary: International World Cup fans are driving viral buzz around American retail and restaurant chains like Walmart, Buc-ee’s, and Waffle House. The 2026 FIFA World Cup is expected to inject $1.47B into US wholesale and retail sectors, per ICSC’s Commerce + Communities Today. Operators near host cities see a surge in foot traffic and spending as visitors post their ‘very American’ experiences on social media.…

items.headline: NYC’s top deals: Brooklyn logs borough-wide record for year with $25M townhouse sale
items.summary: There were 231 transactions totaling $338 million filed in New York City records in the 24 hours before 4 p.m. on Monday, July 6. Commercial: Flushing logged the most expensive commercial deal recorded in New York. Prime Pacific LLC took over a multifamily property at 41-31 Haight Street for $7.5 million amid a foreclosure lawsuit. The mixed-use building stands six stories tall and has 23 apartments; it measures 23,700 square feet. The prior owner was 41 Haight Street Owner, LLC.…

items.headline: Mysterious Manhattan building up for sale for first time in more than a decade
items.summary: RFR developer Aby Rosen, who bought spooky 190 Bowery in a $55 million in 2015, tapped Avison Young’s James Nelson to offer the six-story property with 33,000 square feet of office space.

items.headline: Law Firm Loeb & Loeb Expands to 179K SF at Rudin’s 345 Park Avenue
items.summary: A global law firm is growing its office footprint to 178,959 square feet in Midtown East. Loeb & Loeb , which has more than 450 attorneys across the U.S. and Asia, signed a 16-year extension for its existing 160,051-square-foot offices on the 18th through 21st floors of Rudin ’s 345 Park Avenue and added 18,908 square feet on part of the 13th floor, according to the landlord. Loeb & Loeb has leased its New York City headquarters at the building since 1993.…

items.headline: Anchorage Capital Advisors Takes 21K SF at 125 West 57th Street
items.summary: Billionaires’ Row continues to grow as a premier office destination as a new tenant seeks to move up from Lower Manhattan. Anchorage Capital Advisors , an investment firm with more than $27.8 billion in assets under management, has signed a 10-year, 20,560-square-foot lease at Alchemy-ABR Investment Partners and Cain ‘s 125 West 57th Street , the landlords announced Monday.…

items.headline: Uptown retail booming on Manhattan’s Second Avenue
items.summary: The opening of the Q subway line and rezoning a decade ago propelled a residential development frenzy, including at least seven major projects between East 71st and East 86th streets.

items.headline: Billionaires’ Row tower signs new tenant
items.summary: The deal for two full floors brings the project to nearly 75% leased less than three years since construction began.

items.headline: Former Downtown Brooklyn Macy’s Slated for Dramatic Retail Makeover
items.summary: The 440,000-square-foot space at Downtown Brooklyn’s 422 Fulton Street — the largest block of retail space currently available in New York City and the former site of a Macy’s department store — will become BKX, a five-floor retail and amusement destination that’s being described by developers as a “next-generation cultural and commercial hub,” Commercial Observer has learned.…

items.headline: Fitness Factory to Open 25K-SF Gym at 162-11 Jamaica Avenue in Queens
items.summary: Don’t you know pump it up? You got to pump it up! And residents in Queens are about to get another option for pumping up their gym workout. Fitness Factory , a growing fitness and wellness chain in New York and New Jersey, has signed a 25,000-square-foot lease to open a new gym at 162-11 Jamaica Avenue in Jamaica, Queens, Commercial Observer has learned. The deal was signed June 18, according to a source close to the deal. Fitness Factory will open a four-floor gym at its new space.…

items.headline: Vanbarton reels in $352M refi for nearly full Grand Central office tower
items.summary: Vanbarton Group landed a fresh nine-figure loan for its nearly full Midtown office tower. Goldman Sachs provided a $352 million loan for 425 Lexington Avenue, a 31-story, 747,000-square-foot Class A office building next to Grand Central Terminal, according to property records. A source with knowledge of the deal said the single-property CMBS deal was pre-placed entirely with BlackRock. The debt replaces a $290 million loan from New York Life Insurance Company and Northwestern Mutual.…

items.headline: Azorim secures financing for final phase of Yonkers complex
items.summary: An Israel-based development firm is preparing to put the finishing touches on a massive multifamily complex a dozen miles north of Manhattan. Azorim landed $69 million in construction financing from Western Alliance Bank for the fourth and final building of Mizora at Ridge Hill in Yonkers, the Commercial Observer reported. This component of the master-planned community in the Westchester County city is at 599 Ridge Hill Boulevard.…

items.headline: Sonesta International signs $245M refi with Fortress for four hotels in Manhattan
items.summary: Sonesta International Hotels through the entity Sonesta Benjamin Hotel LLC as borrower signed a refi The post Sonesta International signs $245M refi with Fortress for four hotels in Manhattan appeared first on PincusCo .

items.headline: Brooklyn Heights townhouse trades for $25M
items.summary: A Brooklyn Heights townhouse just nabbed the borough’s priciest sale so far this year. The home at 192 Columbia Heights traded in an off-market deal for $24.5 million, according to public records. The closing price surpassed the year’s previous top sale, a penthouse at Fortis Property Group’s Olympia Dumbo, which sold for $16.3 million in March. The deal for the Civil War-era townhouse fell short of Brooklyn’s overall record, set by Eli Gindi of the Gindi real estate family in November, when he sold his…

items.headline: Former NYC “worst landlord” strikes a deal, avoids foreclosure
items.summary: An infamous New York City landlord and his lender have struck a deal. Ved Parkash, one of the city’s “worst landlords,” has agreed to sell three of his buildings as part of an agreement with Community Stabilization Partners, Gothamist reported. The lender issued an ultimatum requiring the sale of the buildings to prevent foreclosure on those properties, as well as 21 others in his company’s portfolio. The properties slated for sale include a 44-unit property at 1110 Anderson Ave.…

items.headline: Full-floor condo at Central Park Tower snags signed contract, asking $55M
items.summary: A trophy condo at Extell Development’s Central Park Tower nabbed a buyer last week, topping a slow week for luxury deals ahead of the Fourth of July. A full-floor unit at 217 West 57th Street, last asking $54.9 million, was the priciest of just 15 homes in Manhattan asking $4 million or more to enter contract between June 29 and July 5, according to Olshan Realty’s weekly report. The total was down sharply from 27 contracts signed in the previous week.…

items.summary: The storybook residence, a traditional home built in 1936, stands just up the hill from Chateau Marmont, the legendary hotel in West Hollywood.
